blockingIssues.description: server/storage.ts createContact force-casts optional userId and can insert NULL into contacts.user_id, which now violates the NOT NULL constraint at runtime.

server/routes.ts POST /api/contacts (and other createContact callsites) forwards payloads parsed from insertContactSchema without guaranteeing userId, causing runtime failures against contacts.user_id NOT NULL.
server/storage.ts getInteraction reads by interaction id only with no user_id filter, violating required user-scoped query behavior.
server/storage.ts updateInteraction and deleteInteraction operate by id only with no user_id filter, enabling potential cross-user writes/deletes.
server/storage.ts getInteractionBySourceId filters only by (channel, source_id) and does not scope by user_id.

appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/relationshipos-schema-notes.md documenting that contacts.user_id is DB NOT NULL and contact-create paths must always assign userId before storage.createContact.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit exception path for infrastructure/type-only or storage-only tasks so mandatory RED-first and manual endpoint checks can be adapted with written justification.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: setup-test-infrastructure, storage-layer-interactions, google-oauth-auth, and fix-preexisting-typescript-errors reviews all flagged procedure mismatch around strict TDD/manual endpoint expectations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify instruction precedence when a feature explicitly requires fixing pre-existing baseline issues that mission guidance otherwise says not to fix.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: fix-preexisting-typescript-errors review flagged conflict between mission AGENTS "do not fix pre-existing TypeScript errors" guidance and the feature's explicit objective; similar mismatch appeared in google-oauth-auth review context.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a migration guardrail: when adding new NOT NULL foreign keys to legacy entities, either make input schemas required simultaneously or enforce fallback assignment in every write path before rollout.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: schema-users-contacts-interactions review found contacts.user_id DB NOT NULL while insert/schema and create routes still allowed missing userId.

rejectedObservations.observation: storage-layer-interactions note that backend-worker requires manual endpoint checks for storage-only work
google-oauth-auth note about strict RED-first ordering not followed
schema-users-contacts-interactions claim that missing library knowledge alone caused the defect
